title: Create isolated Laravel mobile API route group
goal: Add /api/mobile routes for the React Native app while preserving all existing web frontend API routes.
implementation_steps: Add a Route::prefix('mobile') group in Backend/routes/api.php.
Apply middleware cache.headers and mobile.api.key to the entire mobile group.
Inside the mobile group, define public mobile routes for login and register.
Inside the mobile group, define a nested auth:sanctum group for logout, me, and book lookup.
Use controller names that clearly indicate mobile behavior, preferably MobileAuthController and MobileBookController.
Do not remove, rename, or alter existing non-mobile API routes.
acceptance_criteria: Protected mobile routes require Bearer token.
Existing /api/login and /api/register still work for the current web frontend.
Existing Frontend dashboard behavior is unchanged.
verification: Run php artisan route:list and confirm /api/mobile routes.
Call existing /api/login without x-api-key and confirm it still reaches normal auth behavior.
out_of_scope: Do not implement controller logic in this task unless stubs are needed.
